title: BlackBerry's Q1 2025 Earnings Signal Undervaluation and Growth in Cybersecurity and IoT
text: BlackBerry's Q1 2025 earnings results signal potential undervaluation with significant growth opportunities in cybersecurity and IoT divisions. The company reported total revenue of $144 million, with cybersecurity and IoT divisions contributing 96% of total revenue. IoT revenue surged 18% year-over-year to $53 million, driven by QNX. QNX revenue reached $57.5 million with 8.1% year-over-year growth. The Cybersecurity segment reported $85 million in revenue with ARR of $285 million and 87% dollar-based net retention rate. Adjusted EBITDA was negative $7 million. Free cash usage decreased to $16 million. Total cash and investments were $283 million. The company forecast 2026 revenue of $508-538 million. Despite near-term challenges, the underlying growth in both cybersecurity and IoT divisions suggests the company may be undervalued based on its long-term potential in these high-growth markets.
